task: Use the gripper to push and close the drawer
raw:
SCENE ANALYSIS - A white drawer is open on a table. A red object (likely a handle) is visible on the table to the right of the drawer. The drawer has a front face and a handle (the red object). STRATEGY - First, reach for the drawer's handle (the red object) to grasp it. Then, pull the drawer towards the gripper to close it. Failure modes include not grasping the handle properly or pulling with insufficient force. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1: Reach for the drawer's handle (the red object) to grasp it. Step 2: Grasp the handle to secure the grip. Step 3: Pull the drawer towards the gripper to close it.
Goal: closed(drawer)
Scene: drawer, red_handle, table
Step 1: reach(red_handle)
  affordance_region: the red handle on the table
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, red_handle, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, red_handle) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(red_handle)
  affordance_region: the red handle on the table
  contact: holding(red_handle) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(red_handle)
Step 3: pull(drawer -> gripper)
  affordance_region: the front face of the open drawer
  contact: holding(red_handle) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, drawer, <, 0.1) [completion]
  direction: pull_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, drawer) < 0.1
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the red handle on the table
the red handle on the table
the front face of the open drawer
